title: 七面钟之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\144-七面钟之谜-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Detective Bartholomew discusses the shooting of Mr. Cecil with Sir Oswald, noting that a man fled the scene after dropping a gun. Bartholomew explains that they only learned of the intruder's flight after Mr. Cecil informed them, and he reveals that men are guarding the garden to catch anyone attempting to leave, though no one has been caught yet. Bartholomew also expresses suspicion regarding the abundance of ivy vines at the location.

Sir Oswald is interrupted by his secretary, Rupert Bettman, who informs him that Mrs. Cutt is distressed and fears Sir Oswald might be murdered. Sir Oswald dismisses these concerns and follows Bettman. Meanwhile, Superintendent Bartlett and Jimmy Sycamore discuss Bettman, whom Sycamore describes as smart but lacking a sense of humor. Miss Erin questions why Sir Oswald was wandering the garden at night, but the inspector defends Sir Oswald's character.

The investigation shifts to Miss Wade, who explains she was at the scene because she felt a need for security and had purchased a gun from Harrods. She claims she saw a tall man climbing down the ivy after a paper package landed at her feet. Detective Bartholomew suggests the package was intentionally thrown at an accomplice. Mr. Cecil adds that the assailant was a strong, uneducated man who made growling sounds during a struggle.

Detective Bartholomew discovers Countess Razkai unconscious behind a Spanish leather screen. As she regains consciousness with the help of Bender and Bondel, Bill Everly expresses intense anxiety. The Countess assures the group of her well-being, citing her Hungarian "steel will," and directs a gentle comment toward Bill.
